---
title: "Explore the knowledge graph"
subtitle: "The node & relationship schema, wired up with synthetic example data — click around"
author: "Veronika Palotai"
date: "2026-07-13"
categories: [knowledge-graph, kuzu, visualization]
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
    toc: false
---

The warehouse feeds a **Kuzu** property graph with a `Day` spine that stitches every
domain together — cycle phase, labs, nutrition, activity. Below is that exact schema
(11 node types, 12 relationship types), populated with a small **synthetic** example
day — three logged meals, a lab result, an activity, cycle signs — so you can see how a
real record threads together. **Click any node** to highlight its links (their labels
appear) and inspect its (fake) properties; click the background to reset.

Follow the nutrition chain — `Meal —EATEN→ Food —HAS_NUTRIENT→ Nutrient`: a logged meal
resolves to its canonical USDA food (with the portion in grams), and each food to its
full nutrient profile. That's how the graph knows the *magnesium in the salmon* even
though the food log only ever recorded calories and macros. Every food links to the same
complete nutrient set — 6 shown here, all 16 in the real graph.

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
Every value here is invented. No real measurements appear anywhere on this site — this
is the *structure*, not my data.
:::

```{=html}
<style>
  #graph-wrap { position: relative; width: 100%; height: 600px; border: 1px solid #e6e6e6;
                border-radius: 8px; background: #fbfbfb; overflow: hidden; }
  #cy { width: 100%; height: 100%; }
  #legend { position: absolute; top: 12px; left: 12px; background: rgba(255,255,255,.93);
            border: 1px solid #e6e6e6; border-radius: 6px; padding: 9px 12px; font-size: 12px;
            z-index: 5; max-width: 300px; }
  #legend b { font-size: 11px; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: .04em; color: #555; }
  #legend .row { margin-top: 5px; line-height: 1.5; }
  #legend i { color: #999; font-style: normal; font-size: 10px; text-transform: uppercase;
              letter-spacing: .03em; margin-right: 2px; }
  #legend s { display: inline-block; width: 10px; height: 10px; border-radius: 2px;
              margin: 0 3px 0 7px; vertical-align: -1px; text-decoration: none; }
  #panel { position: absolute; top: 12px; right: 12px; width: 240px; background: rgba(255,255,255,.97);
           border: 1px solid #e6e6e6; border-radius: 6px; padding: 12px 14px; font-size: 13px;
           z-index: 5; display: none; box-shadow: 0 2px 10px rgba(0,0,0,.06); }
  #panel .ptype { font-size: 11px; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: .04em; font-weight: 600; }
  #panel h3 { margin: 4px 0 8px; font-size: 16px; }
  #panel table { width: 100%; border-collapse: collapse; }
  #panel td { padding: 2px 0; vertical-align: top; }
  #panel td.k { color: #888; padding-right: 8px; white-space: nowrap; }
  #reset { position: absolute; bottom: 12px; left: 12px; z-index: 5; font-size: 12px;
           background: #fff; border: 1px solid #ccc; border-radius: 5px; padding: 5px 10px;
           cursor: pointer; color: #333; }
  #hint { position: absolute; bottom: 12px; right: 14px; z-index: 5; font-size: 11px; color: #999; }
</style>

<div id="graph-wrap">
  <div id="cy"></div>
  <div id="legend">
    <b>Node types</b>
    <div class="row"><i>cycle</i><s style="background:#332288"></s>CyclePhase<s style="background:#88CCEE"></s>Symptom</div>
    <div class="row"><i>labs</i><s style="background:#DDCC77"></s>LabResult<s style="background:#CC6677"></s>Biomarker<s style="background:#999933"></s>RefRange</div>
    <div class="row"><i>food</i><s style="background:#882255"></s>Meal<s style="background:#117733"></s>Food<s style="background:#44AA99"></s>Ingredient</div>
    <div class="row"><i>nutrient</i><s style="background:#6A3D9A"></s>energy<s style="background:#9970AB"></s>macro<s style="background:#C2A5CF"></s>micro</div>
    <div class="row"><s style="background:#222222"></s>Day<s style="background:#EE7733"></s>Activity</div>
  </div>
  <div id="panel"></div>
  <button id="reset">Reset view</button>
  <span id="hint">click a node to label its links · drag to pan · scroll to zoom</span>
</div>

<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/cytoscape@3.30.2/dist/cytoscape.min.js"></script>
<script>
(function () {
  // Distinct colour per node type (Paul Tol palette — colourblind-safe).
  var COLOR = {
    Day:"#222222", CyclePhase:"#332288", Symptom:"#88CCEE", Biomarker:"#CC6677",
    LabResult:"#DDCC77", ReferenceRange:"#999933", Meal:"#882255", Food:"#117733",
    Ingredient:"#44AA99", Nutrient:"#AA4499", Activity:"#EE7733"
  };
  function n(id, label, type, props, color) {
    return { data: { id:id, label:label, ntype:type, color: color || COLOR[type] || "#888", props:props } };
  }
  function e(s, t, rel, extra) { return { data: { source:s, target:t, rel:rel + (extra?" · "+extra:"") } }; }

  var nodes = [
    n("day","2025-01-14","Day",{ "cycle day":"14", weekday:"Tuesday", phase:"ovulation" }),
    n("phase","ovulation","CyclePhase",{ "fertility zone":"fertile", "inferred from":"BBT shift + mucus peak + LH surge" }),
    n("sym_m","Cervical mucus","Symptom",{ value:"egg-white", ordinal:"5 (peak fertility)", predicts:"ovulation" }),
    n("sym_lh","LH / ovulation test","Symptom",{ value:"positive (surge)", note:"a hint only — unreliable under PMOS" }),
    n("sym_bbt","Basal body temperature","Symptom",{ value:"36.50 °C", measured:"daily, on waking", note:"the confirmer — a sustained rise dates ovulation" }),
    n("act","Morning run","Activity",{ distance:"5.2 km", duration:"31 min", energy:"410 kcal", source:"Strava" }),
    n("lab","Estradiol — lab result","LabResult",{ value:"1108 pmol/L", date:"2025-01-14", "as-of status":"normal" }),
    n("bm","Estradiol (biomarker)","Biomarker",{ category:"hormone", unit:"pmol/L", key:"estradiol" }),
    n("rr","Estradiol — reference range","ReferenceRange",{ interval:"46–607 pmol/L", "valid from":"2024-02", note:"judged as-of the result's date" }),
    n("ing_milk","Milk","Ingredient",{ "used in foods":"1,204" }),
    n("ing_oats","Oats, dry","Ingredient",{ "used in foods":"188" }),
    n("ing_fat","Table fat","Ingredient",{ "used in foods":"96" })
  ];
  var edges = [
    // cycle: the phase is INFERRED from the symptoms, but in the graph both attach to
    // the Day — there is no direct Symptom→CyclePhase edge (the phase label IS that link).
    e("day","phase","IN_PHASE"),
    e("sym_m","day","OBSERVED_ON","egg-white"),
    e("sym_lh","day","OBSERVED_ON","positive"),
    e("sym_bbt","day","OBSERVED_ON","36.50 °C"),
    e("act","day","PERFORMED_ON"),
    e("lab","day","RESULT_ON"),
    e("lab","bm","MEASURED_AS"),
    e("rr","bm","REF_FOR"),
    // Only genuinely composite foods get a COMPOSED_OF breakdown (oatmeal = milk + oats +
    // fat); whole foods (banana, chicken, salmon) have none — the logged food IS the ingredient.
    e("food_o","ing_milk","COMPOSED_OF","240 g"),
    e("food_o","ing_oats","COMPOSED_OF","40 g"),
    e("food_o","ing_fat","COMPOSED_OF","5 g")
  ];

  // Nutrients split into energy | macro | micro. [key, label, unit, decimals, category].
  var NUTR = [
    ["energy","Energy","kcal",0,"energy"], ["protein","Protein","g",1,"macro"],
    ["fat","Fat","g",1,"macro"], ["carbohydrate","Carbohydrate","g",1,"macro"],
    ["magnesium","Magnesium","mg",0,"micro"], ["iron","Iron","mg",1,"micro"]
  ];
  var MACROS = ["energy","protein","fat","carbohydrate"];  // the set the app logs
  var NCOLOR = { energy:"#6A3D9A", macro:"#9970AB", micro:"#C2A5CF" };
  NUTR.forEach(function (x) {
    nodes.push(n(x[0], x[1], "Nutrient", { category: x[4], unit: x[2] }, NCOLOR[x[4]]));
  });

  // Real USDA foods (public reference data). [id, label, fdc_id, portion g, per-100g in NUTR order].
  var FOODS = [
    ["food_o","Oatmeal, with milk",2708384,200,[128,5.48,4.75,15.95,35,0.67]],
    ["food_b","Banana",173944,120,[89,1.09,0.33,22.84,27,0.26]],
    ["food_c","Chicken breast, roasted",171477,150,[165,31.02,3.57,0,29,1.04]],
    ["food_v","Broccoli, boiled",169967,160,[35,2.38,0.41,7.18,21,0.67]],
    ["food_s","Salmon, cooked",172001,150,[153,24.58,5.28,0,32,0.45]],
    ["food_p","Sweet potato, baked",2709699,200,[82,1.64,0.39,17.98,20,0.42]]
  ];
  var FLABEL = {};
  FOODS.forEach(function (f) {
    FLABEL[f[0]] = f[1];
    var per100 = f[4], grams = f[3], props = { fdc_id: f[2], portion: grams + " g" };
    NUTR.forEach(function (x, i) { props[x[1]] = (per100[i] * grams / 100).toFixed(x[3]) + " " + x[2]; });
    nodes.push(n(f[0], f[1], "Food", props));  // props = the nutrient facts for this portion
    NUTR.forEach(function (x, i) { edges.push(e(f[0], x[0], "HAS_NUTRIENT", per100[i] + " " + x[2] + "/100g")); });
  });

  // Three meals, each a main + a side. Real food names in `items`; logged macro totals on CONTAINS.
  var MEALS = [
    ["m_bf","Breakfast","08:10",["food_o","food_b"],{energy:"240 kcal",protein:"6 g",fat:"3.4 g",carbohydrate:"51 g"}],
    ["m_ln","Lunch","13:05",["food_c","food_v"],{energy:"300 kcal",protein:"50 g",fat:"6 g",carbohydrate:"12 g"}],
    ["m_dn","Dinner","19:40",["food_s","food_p"],{energy:"390 kcal",protein:"40 g",fat:"9 g",carbohydrate:"36 g"}]
  ];
  MEALS.forEach(function (m) {
    nodes.push(n(m[0], m[1], "Meal",
      { logged_at: m[2], items: m[3].map(function (id) { return FLABEL[id]; }).join(" · ") }));
    edges.push(e(m[0], "day", "LOGGED_ON"));
    m[3].forEach(function (fid) {
      var f = FOODS.filter(function (z) { return z[0] === fid; })[0];
      edges.push(e(m[0], fid, "EATEN", f[3] + " g"));
    });
    MACROS.forEach(function (k) { edges.push(e(m[0], k, "CONTAINS", m[4][k])); });
  });
  var DAY_TOTAL = { energy:"930 kcal", protein:"96 g", fat:"18 g", carbohydrate:"99 g" };
  MACROS.forEach(function (k) { edges.push(e("day", k, "INTAKE_ON", DAY_TOTAL[k])); });

  var cy = cytoscape({
    container: document.getElementById("cy"),
    elements: nodes.concat(edges),
    style: [
      { selector: "node", style: {
          "background-color": "data(color)", "label": "data(label)",
          "color": "#222", "font-size": 10, "text-wrap": "wrap", "text-max-width": 92,
          "text-valign": "bottom", "text-margin-y": 3, "width": 24, "height": 24,
          "border-width": 2, "border-color": "#fff" } },
      { selector: 'node[ntype = "Day"]', style: { "width": 46, "height": 46, "font-size": 12, "font-weight": "bold" } },
      { selector: 'node[ntype = "Nutrient"]', style: { "width": 30, "height": 30 } },
      { selector: 'node[ntype = "Meal"]', style: { "width": 30, "height": 30 } },
      { selector: "edge", style: {
          "font-size": 8, "color": "#555", "text-rotation": "autorotate",
          "text-background-color": "#fbfbfb", "text-background-opacity": 1, "text-background-padding": 2,
          "width": 1.3, "line-color": "#d0d0d0", "target-arrow-color": "#d0d0d0",
          "target-arrow-shape": "triangle", "arrow-scale": 0.9, "curve-style": "bezier" } },
      { selector: ".faded", style: { "opacity": 0.07, "text-opacity": 0 } },
      { selector: "node.hi", style: { "border-color": "#333", "border-width": 3 } },
      { selector: "edge.hi", style: { "label": "data(rel)", "line-color": "#333",
          "target-arrow-color": "#333", "width": 2.2, "color": "#222", "z-index": 99 } }
    ],
    layout: { name: "cose", idealEdgeLength: 62, nodeRepulsion: 11000, padding: 30,
              animate: false, componentSpacing: 90, nodeOverlap: 14 },
    wheelSensitivity: 0.2
  });
  // The container can have zero size when cytoscape inits below the fold, so fit()
  // measures against nothing. resize() then fit() — and re-fit once it scrolls into view.
  function frame() { cy.resize(); cy.fit(undefined, 34); }
  cy.one("layoutstop", frame);
  cy.ready(function () { setTimeout(frame, 80); });
  window.addEventListener("load", function () { setTimeout(frame, 120); });
  if (window.IntersectionObserver) {
    var io = new IntersectionObserver(function (en) { if (en[0].isIntersecting) frame(); },
                                      { threshold: 0.15 });
    io.observe(document.getElementById("graph-wrap"));
  }

  var panel = document.getElementById("panel");
  function showPanel(node) {
    var d = node.data(), rows = "";
    for (var k in d.props) rows += '<tr><td class="k">' + k + '</td><td>' + d.props[k] + "</td></tr>";
    panel.innerHTML = '<div class="ptype" style="color:' + d.color + '">' + d.ntype +
      '</div><h3>' + d.label + "</h3><table>" + rows + "</table>";
    panel.style.display = "block";
  }
  cy.on("tap", "node", function (ev) {
    var node = ev.target, hood = node.closedNeighborhood();
    cy.elements().addClass("faded").removeClass("hi");
    hood.removeClass("faded").addClass("hi");
    showPanel(node);
  });
  cy.on("tap", function (ev) {
    if (ev.target === cy) { cy.elements().removeClass("faded hi"); panel.style.display = "none"; }
  });
  document.getElementById("reset").onclick = function () {
    cy.elements().removeClass("faded hi"); panel.style.display = "none";
    cy.animate({ fit: { padding: 34 } }, { duration: 400 });
  };
})();
</script>
```

## How to read it — the chains that make it useful

Everything hangs off the `Day` spine, so cross-domain questions become one traversal.
Two chains are worth following.

**Nutrition — the reconciled link.** A logged `Meal` (breakfast / lunch / dinner) connects
to its canonical USDA `Food`s via **`EATEN`** (the portion in grams — a meal has a main and
its sides), and each `Food` to its per-100g profile via **`HAS_NUTRIENT`**. Building that
`Meal → Food` edge means reconciling a named, cross-lingual log entry to the right USDA row
— exactly what the [food-retrieval study](writeup_food_retrieval.qmd) solves; its `food_map`
output populates `EATEN`. The reward: traverse `Meal —EATEN→ Food —HAS_NUTRIENT→ Nutrient`
and you compute a nutrient the log **never recorded** — a day's magnesium — as
`Σ portion/100 × the food's per-100g amount`. Every food links to the *same* full nutrient
set (generated from the USDA data — no cherry-picked gaps).

**Cycle — inference, not a shortcut edge.** You asked whether `Symptom` should link to
`CyclePhase`. In the model it doesn't, on purpose: the phase is *inferred from* the daily
cycle signs — cervical mucus predicts ovulation, the **basal body temperature (BBT)**
confirms it, the LH test is a hint — so both the signs and the resulting phase attach to the
same `Day` (`Symptom —OBSERVED_ON→ Day —IN_PHASE→ CyclePhase`). The inferred `phase` on the
`Day` **is** that relationship; a direct edge would duplicate it. Click **ovulation** and
trace back through the day to the egg-white mucus, positive LH test, and the 36.50 °C BBT that
inferred it. (BBT is a `Symptom` too — a signed daily reading on the `OBSERVED_ON` edge.)

A couple of modelling notes the picture invites:

- **Nutrients split into energy / macro / micro** (the three purple shades). All are one node
  *type* — the quantity lives on the **edge**, not the node: `Lunch —CONTAINS·50 g→ Protein`
  (logged), `Chicken —HAS_NUTRIENT·31 g/100g→ Protein` (USDA). The `energy`/`macro` nutrients
  are what the app logs; the `micro` ones (magnesium, iron) connect *only* to foods — never
  the meal — because the log never recorded them; they're reached through the food link.
- **Ingredients are sparse, on purpose.** `COMPOSED_OF` appears only for genuinely composite
  foods — oatmeal made with milk resolves to *milk + oats + fat*. A logged banana, chicken
  breast, or salmon has none: the logged food *is* the ingredient, so there's nothing to
  decompose. That unevenness is a faithful artifact of a mixed food log, not a gap.
- **`Estradiol` is connected to its result** — `LabResult —MEASURED_AS→ Biomarker`, judged
  against `ReferenceRange —REF_FOR→ Biomarker` in effect on the result's date.
